item.content:encoded: A massive milestone. This is my favorite SCP I’ve written for a variety of reasons, and I’m thrilled to see it thrive on the site.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: As was said on the first page, a keter almost never has a mere two sentence containment procedure. That actually piqued my interest immediately. Got me thinking, how could that be keter? And boy was it excellent.

item.title: Request to Reclassify as Thaumiel
item.content:encoded:
Device may have its flaws, but it has prevented at least ██ XK Class end of world scenarios. Our attempts to dismantle it after it attacked a site director with no clear reasoning as far as we can see were rash and short-sighted after the positive effects it has had.

-Dr. ██████

[I kinda halfheartedly decided to do that sorta in character. I like it though, solid! haha.]
Fantastic! Out of all the SCPs I've written, this remains my personal favorite - I was firing on all cylinders when I wrote this and I think it shows. Onwards to 300!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Reread this again, and man - what a fucking delight. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: A Hindu laser that kills criminals through cause and effect? Shut up and take my +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Finally got around to reading this and damn, it was worth the wait! I can see why you consider it your best work ARD, the anomaly is extremely interesting and the plot and character writing on point. Hearty +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The reason this works so well to me is that Minority Report-style "they were always going to be a criminal" scifi and the ethical debates around it are so, so tired. So I really liked that 1. the weapon kills people in ridiculous ways and 2. the AI doesn't go on about the cruel necessity of its actions to preserve law and order, but rather has been hard-programmed into deluding itself to think it's a Hindu god. What's more, the AI's delusions are thematically consistent. Easy +1.

item.title: Re: Om
item.content:encoded:
+1 to that sentiment! (If I could vote on comments)

I was thinking of doing something with one of the concepts (astras), but it looks like you beat me to it, and I'm glad you did!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Which I'm kind of iffy about. There's a lot of text that puts it out of character for any avatar of Vishnu. And then there are other details that are supposed to apply to the 10th avatar that don't apply to the SCP.

Still, it adds a lot of character to the page, so I enjoyed it!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is great. I can see why you picked it as a personal favorite among your works. It's just awesome.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'd actually agree that it sounds more like a Thaumiel than a Keter, despite having targeted Foundation personnel, but that's a really either/or sort of classification.

I…actually have to downvote this personally, for a couple reasons that may mostly just be my beefs. It's all in the event logs though.

Target 79
Target 79

1) This redaction just kills me. It KILLS me. The redactions in this one make it feel like a yakety-sax joke, which is not helped by things like 'the frog detonates the bomb' and 'rock falls out of pocket, breaks tequila, prevents XK scenario'. Without redactions…I don't know how it would read. With them, it just parses as a badly written joke to me.

2)

07:16 (18/09): Laws of gravity briefly cease working in a cylinder around the top of the dam, causing the Homo superior to asphyxiate.
08:34 (18/09): Laws of gravity reinstated around top of dam.

I'm hoping you just did more research on this than I did, because this is, uh. First, even LOCALIZED cessation of gravity entirely seems pretty likely to cause IMMENSE collateral damage, especially when it lasts for over an hour. Secondly, gravity going whack seems a weird and out-of-place way to cause someone to suffocate. The Homo superior part bothers me as well, but that's an obvious bit of fallout from the redactions that I dislike. The gravity bit though is…kind of a huge deal in science.
Additionally, LOCALIZED ALTERATION OF GRAVITY seems a pretty monstrously intense effect caused by this sort of Rube Goldberg god-laser. Once again, either the redaction is hiding how this could POSSIBLY happen - see above beefs with redaction - or this is wildly out of the potential scope of the 'butterfly effect' kind of chain of events.

Target 91
Target 91

This one's a pretty small thought, but again, it kinda seems a bit ridiculous that this god-laser can…let's be real, CREATE, not exploit, a defect in something like the SRA's. The canon on them is a bit fuzzy, so this is fairly easily ignorable, but they generally are understood well enough that a defect that could, uh…create its own reality-destroying effect probably nearing an ?K-class event should not really be super feasible. It would actually make more sense for one of them to malfunction and just literally explode and kill him.

While the test logs kinda kill it for me, your tone is amazing, your writing is spectacular, and I LOVE the AI itself. Real or programmed, it's got itself lined up PROPER and knows exactly what it is/thinks it is.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This could sound like a nice Thaumiel. Too bad it also targets Foundation personnel for vague reasons.

My third SCP to break the +100 barrier! And in just four months!
I'm really glad this has been so well received; I think it's some of my best work to date.
Be sure to check out Third Law for more science fiction goodness!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Hit monkey no longer finds joy in his profession.

This is by far one of the weirdest things I've read in a long time. A chimp in a trench coat who operates as an avatar of Vishnu to stop SCPs that can't be otherwise contained? +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yes.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
ecto-copulation

Is that possession by a non-corporeal entity? Sex with a non-corporeal entity? Both?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Dang, this is something I wouldn't expect. Causing anomalous chains of events to happen to kill something as powerful as an XK-class type-green anomaly in either a very silly fashion, or a very dreadful way.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I mean, it fits no matter, what, right? He's a son of Vayu, really loves to hang out with Rama, who is a different avatar of Vishnu just like Kalki himself, except a different, far older avatar, unable to move on.

There's an interpretation that he's an avatar of Shiva, but I find difficulty in assuming you're going for that since he's playing a passive role in this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It might be… Very good catch! You certainly know your stuff!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The chimp is Hanuman, isn't it?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
hey, a keter that… helps?

Neat. I like it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+1

Two notes, though.

  1. Typo: " Due to the sensitive information contained in tis document"
  2. Slightly irreverent tone on the part of the researchers in a few locations; fixing this would go a long way toward making it feel more pedantic. E.g. usage of "On accident" rather than "By accident" or "Accidentally" or even "Inadvertantly." "On accident" tends to be a more youthful and less professional term. However, that's only my opinion.

Overall, I enjoyed this article, and the misguided A.I.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's not based off anything IRL; it's almost but not quite similar to Hindu yajnas, which were fire-based rituals often used by kings so that they could have powerful children. In-universe the ritual basically seeks to birth a demigod by the sexual union of a mortal and a divine spirit. It would have worked too, but 2820's intervention caused the ritual to fail and produce a deformed, mentally disabled parody of a demigod that was quickly dispatched.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Is the ritual based off of something?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Made some adjustments based on your feedback, but the chimp stays connected to the death ray.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Way better the second time 'round. Couple nitpicks though:

(ranging from falling bricks to a person missing their bus)

Not really an issue, more of a personal preference, but I feel this could be better if you used two examples that don't play out later on in the article anyway. If it's so chaotic and random, this would be a good opportunity to quickly reference two more unique manifestations.

Also, if the series of events is initiated/completed within 24 hours, you might need to tweak your Target 79 log, as the events go on longer than that.

Personal preference time again:

PL%default: how does this machine work? what is with the chimpanzee in a trench coat?

I think it'd be great way to further introduce ambiguity if the gun to had no friggen' idea what he was talking about! :D

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Whoa. You really took this to a whole new level. I liked it before, but this is a step up in just about every regard. Take all of my +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Excellent SCP, though I have one critique. In the first interview with the SCP, the researcher asks, "what is with the chimpanzee in a trench coat?"

That sounds far too casual in this context, even considering the subject matter.

Other than that, well done!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I think the P part should not be so short.

but this looks so nice.

and, William Bell from Fringe?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Wow, this is a really odd mesh of various bits and bobs. Space lasers, Hindu mythology, Prometheus labs, the butterfly effect, and a sassy AI. Even more impressive than this eclectic mix of concepts is that they actually gel pretty well to create a fun SCP article.

+1 ARD. If at first you do not succeed, try and try again. You really hit it out of the park on the second time around.

item.title: +50 in under 24 hours
item.content:encoded: That's a new personal best!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
What the hell.

A Keter should not have a two-line containment procedure. That just shouldn't happen. I mean, there's not even any indication that they should be monitoring who the next target is to, I don't know, minimize collateral damage?

Yet in the end, it doesn't matter, because this is wholly outlandish and really works.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Jinotega, man…

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Technically, the SCP identifies itself as Kalki, the tenth avatar of Vishnu.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like to think it actually is Shiva. The idea of mythological figures using/manifesting as technology is really interesting.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Makes me wonder… Was this intended to be a space exploration AI before it was introduced to Hinduism and deemed itself Kalki OR it was really Kalki?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You had me at "Chimpanzee in a trenchcoat".

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I loved this the first time, and I'm still loving it now. I like how it's left ambiguous as to whether this really is a reincarnation of Shiva, or if the computer is a bit too much in-character.

By the way, have you read Supergod by Warren Ellis? I'm getting some of those vibes from this article and it's a good thing.

+1

item.title: Om
item.content:encoded:
Take 2.

Many thanks to everyone who provided feedback the first time around.

Special thanks to TwistedGears (who routinely takes my passable drafts and forces me to make them good), Athenodora (who helped me brainstorm new ways to take the draft), Eguss (who went out of his way to offer crit and suggested the final log), and Michael Atreus (from whom I took the comm log formatting - go read SCP-2409).

Hinduism is cool. There's action, romance, honor, friendship, and so much more. It's far older, far richer, and far denser than any Judeo-Christian or even Greek religion (seriously, the Ramayana is four times longer than the Odyssey and Iliad combined!). Go read up on Hinduism and write some SCPs about it!

See more of my work on my author page!
